chief_complaint: A 6-year-old boy presents with diffuse purpura, arthralgia, and acute abdominal pain.
history: Henoch-Schonlein purpura (HSP) is a self-limiting disease that often follows a viral infection. The patient was treated with oral corticosteroids but showed no improvement. He then developed hematemesis and massive intestinal hemorrhage.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: diffuse purpura, arthralgia, and acute abdominal pain.
correct_diagnosis: Henoch-Schonlein purpura with severe gastrointestinal involvement
differentials: Henoch-Schonlein purpura with severe gastrointestinal involvement
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to other cause
correct_ranking: Henoch-Schonlein purpura with severe gastrointestinal involvement
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to other cause
